text: The URL routing implementation in Django spans several key files: (1) `django/urls/resolvers.py` is the main dispatcher containing URLResolver (which processes urlpatterns lists, resolves URLs to views, and handles both legacy regex and modern route patterns), URLPattern, RegexPattern, RoutePattern, and ResolverMatch. Its `url_patterns` property reads the `urlpatterns` list from URL conf modules. (2) `django/urls/conf.py` contains the `include()` function, the `_path()` factory function, and the `path`/`re_path` partial functions that create URLPattern/URLResolver instances. (3) `django/urls/base.py` provides the top-level `resolve()` entry point that delegates to URLResolver. (4) `django/urls/__init__.py` re-exports the public API. (5) `django/conf/urls/__init__.py` re-exports `include` and defines default error handlers. (6) `django/conf/urls/i18n.py` provides `i18n_patterns()` which creates URLResolver instances with LocalePrefixPattern. The old `patterns()` function was removed in Django 1.10 and no longer exists in this codebase.
